indicators.indicator_label: Reports of suspected cholera outbreak e.g. from expert health KII

indicators.indicator_label: Reports of increasing health needs e.g. from expert health KII
indicators.metric: Qualitative reports of an increase in health needs in the community (e.g. overcrowded health facilities, community events or normal activities of the community disrupted due to illnesses, increased number of deaths in the community, etc.)

indicators.indicator_label: Overall infectious disease incidence (all cause and all age)
indicators.metric: # of consulations/treatments for infectious diseases per 1000 people per epiweek

indicators.indicator_label: Any health needs in past three months

indicators.indicator_label: Qualitative reports on inavailability of food
indicators.metric: Qualitative reports that key food sources are depleted or extremely low (local crops destroyed, flooded, uncultivated OR markets without food), and/or alterative food sources are mainly providing irregular amounts of food to the area (wild foods, aid, etc.)

indicators.indicator_label: Qualitative reports on depleted food stocks
indicators.metric: Qualitative reports on food stocks (local or imported crops, warehouses stocks, etc.) at area level being depleted or (rural areas) that households' stocks are depleted (most often local crops).

indicators.indicator_label: Use of surface water in food preparation
indicators.metric: Qualitative reports of surface water use for food preparation

Qualitative reports of people (reasonable share per unit of analysis) not eating for a few days (2-4 days) visible due to overall catastrophic conditions within the unit of analysis. Other context- specific indications can include: people tying a rope or piece of cloth around their waist to stop hunger pangs, extreme reliance on wild foods, or extreme reliance on social networks (incl. begging)

indicators.indicator_label: Qualitative reports of mainly children eating
indicators.metric: Qualitative reports indicating that adults within a household are skipping meals or reducing their food consumption to favor children's or pregnant and lactating women's food consumption

indicators.indicator_label: Qualitative reports on main food groups consumed
indicators.metric: Qualitative reports on 1-2 food groups remaining (staple cereals, pulses or else) in the area, possibly only through in-kind assistance, hot meals distribution or wild foods gathering

indicators.metric: % of HHs reporting they use less preferred, unimproved or unsafe water sources to cope with lack of drinking water

indicators.metric: % of HHs reporting reducing water usage for hygiene to cope with lack of water

indicators.metric: % of HHs reporting [other severe context specific coping] to cope with lack of water
indicators.msna_indicator: % of households engaging in coping mechanisms due water insufficiency, by type of coping mechanism

indicators.indicator_label: Qual reports of stressed availability of water at HH level
indicators.metric: Indications that MOST of the popluation may be experiencing household level stress on availability of water at the household level (inadequate storage for water preventing adequate water consumption, water service interruptions, etc.)
% of HHs with travel and queue time to water source more than 30 minutes
indicators.msna_indicator: % of households by time (minutes) taken to fetch water (round trip by walking, queuing and time needed to fetch water) (W3/W4)

indicators.metric: % of HHs spending more than 5% of income on water
indicators.msna_indicator: Household expenditures in the 30 days prior to data collection, by amount and % per type

indicators.metric: % of HHs reporting physical barriers to accessing water points
indicators.msna_indicator: % of households having had problems related to access to water, by type of problem

indicators.metric: % of HHs reporting financial access barriers to accessing water points
indicators.msna_indicator: % of households having had problems related to access to water, by type of problem

indicators.indicator_label: Qual reports of extreme water coping strategies
indicators.metric: Indications that MOST of the population may be resorting to extreme coping behaviours to meet their daily water needs (spending significant daily time to fetch water each day, using unsafe or contaminated water sources, displacement due to lack of water, etc.)

indicators.indicator_label: Qual reports of limited physical access to water
indicators.metric: Indications that MOST of the popluation may be facing serious physical access constraints to accessing their main drinking water sources (insecurity or lack of safety on the way to or at the water point, flooding or other natural hazard cutting off access, etc.)

indicators.indicator_label: Qual reports of limited financial access to water
indicators.metric: Indications that some of the popluation may be facing serious financial access constraints to accessing their main drinking water sources (extreme price increases double or triple the normal price, selling of assets to afford water, etc.)
% of households with at least two water containers (one for storage and one for collection)
% HH treating water
indicators.msna_indicator: % of households by water treatment method (XW14)

indicators.indicator_label: Cost of water containers, treatment materials, or other items related to water utilization in the market
indicators.metric: % change in price of water containers

indicators.indicator_label: Cost of water containers, treatment materials, or other items related to water utilization in the market
indicators.metric: % change in water treatment materials

indicators.indicator_label: Cost of water containers, treatment materials, or other items related to water utilization in the market
indicators.metric: Water containers not available in market

indicators.indicator_label: Cost of water containers, treatment materials, or other items related to water utilization in the market
indicators.metric: Water treatment materials not available in market

indicators.indicator_label: Qual reports of struggles with water storage
indicators.metric: Indications that MOST of the popluation may be facing serious challenges with water utilization due to lack of water storage capacity (water consumption and use limited by number of containers, utilizing unclean/contaminated or broken water containers, lack of water containers in the market, etc.) and that it is putting them at heightened risk for water borne disease transmission

indicators.indicator_label: Qual reports of struggles with water treatment
indicators.metric: Indications that MOST of the popluation may be facing serious challenges with water utilization due to lack of options to treat water (drinking unsafe water directly, lack of fuel to boil water, etc.) and that it is putting them at heightened risk for water borne disease transmission

indicators.indicator_label: Qual reports of populations struggling to meet water needs
indicators.metric: Indications that most of the population is struggling to access their minimum water needs. (e.g. reported long queues, fighting at water points, spikes in drinking water prices, use of livelihood coping strategies to buy water)

indicators.indicator_label: Qual reports of poor water quality
indicators.metric: Indications that MOST of the population may be reliant on drinking water sources that are exposed to some contamination (e.g. sources of contamination near or at water point including human or animal feces, dead animals, trash or other solid waste, flood waters or other standing/stangant water, etc.)

Indications that MOST of the population may be living in inadequate shelter that either (a) is not durable or sturdy (b) does not provide adequate privacy (c) does not provide safety or protection from the weather/elements (d) does not provide safe place to sleep
% of people sleeping under an insecticide treated bed net the previous night
% change in cost of mosquito nets
No mosquito nets available in market

indicators.indicator_label: Qualitative reports of exposure to disease vectors
indicators.metric: Indications that some of the population may have increased exposure to disease vectors due to inadequate shelter or the environment. This may include: (a) standing water as mosquito breeding grounds (b) poor ventilation of shelters(c) contamined or waste water near shelter(d) rats, pests, or other disease carriers

Indications that MOST of the population may be overcrowded and may be facing increased risks for respiratory disease, protection concerns, or mental health distress. Some indications may include:(a) multiple households sharing shelter (b) people cramped into small shared sleeping spaces(c) not all household members can lie down or fit in the sleeping space(d) reports of widespread coughing or respiratory issues (e) reports of tensions or fighting between neighbours due to proximity and lack of privacy (f) long lines or waits for communal facilities such as latrines, water, etc.
Average covered living space per person
% of HHs with at least 3.5 m2 of covered living space per person
